name: Greenworks 40V 17" Cordless Push Lawn Mower with 4Ah Battery
whatWorks: Battery and charger are included in the box — a key advantage over EGO and Ryobi at this price tier, where tool-only listings are common. What you see is what you pay.
At 17" and ~50 lb, this is one of the lighter cordless mowers available. Maneuvering around garden beds, trees, and tight corners is noticeably easier than heavier 21" alternatives.
The 40V brushless motor handles standard residential grass at a pace matching the 35–45 min runtime. For a typical small urban Canadian lot (under 2,000 sq ft), one charge is usually enough.
Quiet enough to use early morning or in the evening without angering neighbours — noticeably quieter than gas and comparable to most battery alternatives.
worthKnowing: The 17" cutting width means significantly more passes than a 21" deck — on a 3,000+ sq ft lot, the extra time adds up and may exhaust the battery before finishing.
Push-only with no self-propel — on anything but flat ground, you're providing all the motive force. Not ideal for sloped Canadian properties.
The 35–45 min runtime is honest for most users, but heavy or wet grass will shorten it. Don't plan on a single charge covering more than a small lot.
The Greenworks 40V ecosystem is smaller than DEWALT 20V or EGO 56V — fewer compatible tools if you want to expand into blowers, trimmers, and snow blowers later.

commonComplaints.title: 17 inch deck requires so many…
commonComplaints.body: 17 inch deck requires so many passes on my medium-sized yard. Takes almost twice as long as my old 21-inch gas mower.

commonComplaints.title: No self-propel makes slopes exhausting…
commonComplaints.body: No self-propel makes slopes exhausting. My front yard has a gentle slope and pushing this uphill for 40 minutes is a workout.

commonComplaints.title: Battery died before I finished…
commonComplaints.body: Battery died before I finished my yard — had to wait an hour to charge and finish. My yard isn't huge but the 17-inch deck needs more passes.

commonComplaints.title: Struggles with thick wet grass…
commonComplaints.body: Struggles with thick wet grass after a rainy week. Had to slow way down and it still bogged in the heavy spots.

verdict: The Greenworks 40V 17" is the best value entry point into cordless mowing — battery and charger included at $399 CAD beats most competitors at this price. The 17" deck and push-only drive are real limitations that will frustrate anyone with a medium or larger lot, and the 40V ecosystem has fewer expansion options than EGO or DEWALT. For small, flat urban lots under 2,000 sq ft, it's a practical, affordable choice. For anything larger or sloped, the EGO LM2156SP is worth the premium.

realBuyers.quote: Perfect for my small backyard. Battery and charger included — I was mowing the same day it arrived.
My neighbours always comment on how quiet it is compared to their gas mowers.
Lightweight and easy to push. My wife uses it without any trouble — something that wasn't possible with the old gas mower.
For the price and the fact the battery is included, you can't argue with it for a small yard.

sources.title: Best Budget Cordless Lawn Mowers — Consumer Reports
Greenworks Mower Review — This Old House

metaDescription: Greenworks 40V 17" cordless push mower review for Canada: battery included, lightweight, quiet. Honest on 17" deck limitation and no self-propel. ~$399 CAD.
introText: The Greenworks 40V 17" is a push cordless mower that includes battery and charger — making it the most complete package at its price point. It's sized for small urban lots and priced for first-time battery-mower buyers. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07YNKC4LQ) at around $399 CAD.
